zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Heyward於1428年6月16日出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾坎布利其時，她的父親John Howard 1st Duke of Norfolk6歲，母親Catherine Moleyns Duchess of Norfolk6歲。她於1448年與John Oswald在英國格盧斯特斯爾坎布利其結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1500年在她的家鄉享年73歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Heyward于1428年6月16日出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾坎布利其时，她的父亲John Howard 1st Duke of Norfolk6岁，母亲Catherine Moleyns Duchess of Norfolk6岁。她于1448年与John Oswald在英国格盧斯特斯爾坎布利其结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1500年在她的家乡享年73岁。

nameMeanings.name: Heyward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Hayward
meanings: occupational name from Middle English hayward ‘keeper of the hedges and enclosures’ (Old English hēgweard), a manorial officer in charge of the common fields who protected corn and hay from cattle, theft, or damage, and supervised the harvest.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Heyward was born on 16 June 1428, in Cambridge, Gloucestershire, England, her father, John Howard 1st Duke of Norfolk, was 6 and her mother, Catherine Moleyns Duchess of Norfolk, was 6. She married John Oswald in 1448, in Cambridge, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died about 1500, in her hometown, at the age of 73.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Heyward est née le 16 juin 1428, à Cambridge, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, John Howard 1st Duke of Norfolk, avait 6 ans et sa mère, Catherine Moleyns Duchess of Norfolk, avait 6 ans. Elle a épousé John Oswald en 1448, à Cambridge, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1500, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 73 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Heyward am 16. Juni 1428 in Cambridge, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Howard 1st Duke of Norfolk 6 und ihre Mutter Catherine Moleyns Duchess of Norfolk 6. Sie hat 1448 in Cambridge, Gloucestershire, England John Oswald geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1500 im Alter von 73 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Heyward è nata il il 16 giugno 1428 nel Cambridge, Gloucestershire, England, sua padre, John Howard 1st Duke of Norfolk, aveva 6 anni e sua madre, Catherine Moleyns Duchess of Norfolk, aveva 6 anni. Lei sposato John Oswald nel 1448, nel Cambridge, Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì circa 1500, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 73 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Heywardが1428年6月16日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアカンブリッジで生まれた時，父John Howard 1st Duke of Norfolkは6歳，母Catherine Moleyns Duchess of Norfolkは6歳であった。1448年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアカンブリッジでJohn Oswaldと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1500年に故郷で73歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Heyward(이)가 1428년 6월 16일 영국 그루스스터실르 캄브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Howard 1st Duke of Norfolk(은)는 6세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Catherine Moleyns Duchess of Norfolk(은)는 6세였다. 그녀는 1448년 영국 그루스스터실르 캄브리지에서 John Oswald(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1500년 그녀의 고향에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Heyward nasceu em 16 de junho de 1428, em Cambridge, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Howard 1st Duke of Norfolk, tinha 6 anos e sua mãe, Catherine Moleyns Duchess of Norfolk, tinha 6 anos. Ela casou-se com John Oswald em 1448, em Cambridge, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1500, em sua cidade natal, com 73 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Heyward родилась 16 июня 1428 г. в местности камбридж, глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Howard 1st Duke of Norfolk было 6 лет, а матери, которую звали Catherine Moleyns Duchess of Norfolk, 6. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Oswald в 1448 г. в местности камбридж, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1500 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 73 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Heyward nació el 16 de junio de 1428, en Cambridge, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Howard 1st Duke of Norfolk, tenía 6 años y su mamá, Catherine Moleyns Duchess of Norfolk, tenía 6 años. Se casó con John Oswald en 1448, en Cambridge, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1500, en su pueblo, a la edad de 73 años.
